title: MIDDAG React Block Types
description: Schema describing all available block types, their data shapes, and layout regions. Used by AI to generate PageContracts.

type.description: Block type key used in PageContract

regions.description: Layout regions where this block can be placed

dataShape.description: JSON schema of the block's data property

options.description: Customization options available in the block's meta property

blocks.description: Sortable data table with pagination, filters, row actions, and bulk selection. Primary data display block.

dataShape.columns: DataTableColumn[] — key, label, sortable?, hideable? (default true), visible? (default true), minWidth?, variant? (status|boolean|timestamp|progress|link|actions|rich_status|html|link_group), timestampFormat? (relative|datetime|date|time|iso — applies when variant=timestamp)
dataShape.progressCell: when variant=progress the cell value is { value, max?, label?, appearance? } (a bare number is treated as a percent out of 100)
dataShape.rows: Record<string, unknown>[] — each row keyed by column.key
dataShape.rowActions?: { id, label, intent?, icon?, variant?: button|icon }[]

options.columnVisibility: toolbar dropdown toggles hideable columns; a density toggle switches row height. Both are client-only view prefs, persisted across Inertia history navigation when the block descriptor sets remember: true.

blocks.description: KPI card with value, delta, and trend icon. Used in dashboard metrics region.
Health overview with score and status items. Compact status display for aside panels.
Sectioned key-value detail view with field kinds (text, status, timestamp, boolean, link, code, email).

dataShape.DetailField: { key, label, value, kind?: text|status|timestamp|boolean|link|code|email, copyable?, href? }

options.copyableText: string — enables 'Copy Report' button with this text

blocks.description: Chronological event timeline with actors, actions, and expandable details.

dataShape.entries: { id, actor, action, timestamp, details?, icon?, tone? }[]

blocks.description: Schema-driven form with react-hook-form + Zod client validation and Inertia server submission.

dataShape.schema: FormSchemaNode[] — recursive tree of field, section, group, header nodes
dataShape.FormFieldNode: { kind: field, key, component, props: { label, placeholder?, helpText?, required?, validation?, documentType?, documentScope?, defaultCountry?, fixedCountry?, currency?, autocompleteHref?, entityAvatarField?, entitySubtitleField?, visible_when?, hidden_when?, ... } }
dataShape.meta: { submitLabel?, cancelLabel?, cancelHref?, validation?: client | server | both }

options.components: text | email | url | textarea | password | select | multiselect | radio | int | float | checkbox | switch | date | datetime | phone | document | currency | color | slug | tags | entity_picker | file | static | header | hidden

blocks.description: Grid of cards with icon, title, and metadata. Used for connector/integration listings.
Grid of action buttons/cards that trigger server operations.

dataShape.actions: { id, label, description?, icon?, intent?: primary|danger, endpoint, method?, confirmMessage? }[]

blocks.description: Navigation link list with icons and descriptions.
Tabbed container with nested blocks per tab.

dataShape.defaultTab: string — key of initially active tab

blocks.description: Placeholder for empty data states with optional action button.
Rendered markdown content with syntax highlighting and copy buttons.
Nested AND/OR condition tree for rule builders (segments, workflows).
Natural language segment rule builder. Renders rules as human-readable sentences.

dataShape.groups: { id, rules: { id, field, operator, value }[] }[]

blocks.description: Visual workflow editor with nodes and edges (React Flow).
Drag-and-drop form builder with field palette and preview.
Linear workflow state indicator with timestamps. Shows ordered states and highlights the current one.

dataShape.states: { key, label, timestamp? }[] — ordered list of workflow states
dataShape.currentState: string — key of the currently active state

blocks.description: Multi-series chart supporting bar, line, area, and pie chart types. Uses Recharts under the hood (lazy-loaded, ~555 KB).

dataShape.data: Record<string, unknown>[] — data points with category key + numeric values per series
dataShape.series: { key, label, color?, type? }[] — series definitions

options.x-ai-use-when: Analytics dashboards, trend visualization, data comparison

blocks.description: Drag-and-drop kanban board with columns and cards. Cards can be moved between columns via Inertia endpoint. Uses @dnd-kit (lazy-loaded, ~15 KB).

dataShape.columns: { id, title, cards: KanbanCard[] }[] — board columns
dataShape.moveEndpoint: string? — Inertia POST URL for card move operations

options.x-ai-use-when: Task management, pipeline views, status boards
options.x-ai-avoid-when: Simple lists (use dense_table) or non-draggable views (use card_grid)

blocks.description: Editable row × column data matrix (virtualized grid). Cells are edited in place and saved as a batch with an optimistic-concurrency revision.

dataShape.columns: { id, label, type, editable, options? }[] — grid columns
dataShape.cells: { rowId, columnId, value: number|string|null }[] — cell values
dataShape.saveEndpoint?: string — Inertia POST URL for the changed-cell batch save
dataShape.editableStructure?: boolean — allow add/remove of rows and columns

blocks.description: Drag-drop canvas of chart | metric | table widgets on a fixed column grid. The layout persists as a full { revision, widgets } snapshot.

dataShape.widgets: { id, type: chart|metric|table, layout: { x, y, w, h }, title?, chart?, metric?, table? }[]
dataShape.saveEndpoint?: string — Inertia POST URL for the snapshot save
dataShape.editable?: boolean — false renders a static report (no chrome)

blocks.description: Lexical WYSIWYG document editor. content is opaque serialized editor state; the client never emits HTML. Uses Lexical (lazy-loaded, ~300 KB).

dataShape.editable?: boolean — false renders the editor locked
dataShape.saveEndpoint?: string — Inertia POST URL for { revision, content }
dataShape.previewEndpoint?: string — server-rendered HTML preview (adds Write/Preview tabs)

blocks.description: Event calendar across month, week, agenda and resource-lane views. Every change POSTs immediately (save-on-drop) with an optimistic-concurrency revision.

dataShape.events: { id, title, start, end, allDay?, resourceId?, color?, href?, editable?, recurrence? }[] — start/end are ISO strings
dataShape.resources?: { id, label, color? }[] — enable the resource-lanes view
dataShape.view?: month | week | agenda | resources — initial view
dataShape.date?: string — ISO date the view centers on
dataShape.createEndpoint?: string — enables the add-event dialog + empty-slot clicks
